question: Which phrase means 'to criticise someone aggressively'?
I _____ eat a lot of chips and burgers, but now I eat healthy food. (past habit — ended)
Which adjective describes a person who 'behaves properly and treats others with respect'?
Read: 'In England during the 1800s, many people came to the cities to work.' What does this tell us about life in 19th-century England?
choices: people preferred to live in the countryside
there was a major movement of people from rural areas to cities for work
cities were quiet and had few people

question: She _____ be the best player in the team, but now she prefers swimming. (past state — ended)
Which phrase means 'to treat someone with respect and admiration, regarding them as a great role model'?
It took ages to get a connection, _____ was really irritating. (comment on the whole clause)
Which phrase means 'to discuss someone negatively behind their back'?
Read: 'I would argue that families generally remain as close as they ever were.' This phrase introduces:
choices: the writer's personal opinion or thesis
an impersonal view accepted by everyone

question: A Chemist is a person _____ works in a laboratory.
Which word means 'a brother or sister'?
She _____ always arguing with her brother when they were teenagers.
Which phrase signals an IMPERSONAL view in an opinion essay?
Rewrite: 'These headphones stopped working after two days. I paid a fortune for them.' (Combine using relative clause)
choices: These headphones, which I paid a fortune for, stopped working after two days.
These headphones, who I paid a fortune for, stopped working after two days.
These headphones, whose I paid a fortune for, stopped working after two days.
These headphones, that I paid a fortune for, stopped working after two days.

question: Which phrase means 'to display great skill or wealth in order to impress people'?
Aunt Abeer proved that she is _____ of making a real change to young people's lives.
Read: 'But his brother, a man called Monks, knew that Oliver would only get the money if Oliver was an honest person.' Why did Monks arrange for Oliver to meet Fagin?
choices: to help Oliver find a good job
to ensure Oliver would never be honest and could never claim the money
because Monks wanted Oliver to leave the country
because Monks and Fagin were business partners

question: He _____ turn up late and expect everyone to wait for him. (typical annoying present habit)
Which adjective describes someone who is 'very strongly committed to a cause they care about'?
The beach _____ they go to in summer is their favourite place.
My parents _____ always be taking photos of us as children.
Which phrase means 'to behave as a role model others can look up to'?
Read: 'There's a tendency for elderly people to be suspicious of the Internet.' What does 'there's a tendency for' mean?
choices: it is absolutely always the case
it is generally or often the case
it is specifically about one person

question: The quality issues _____ affected the company's earlier models have been solved in the new model.
Which phrase means 'to make someone feel very uncomfortable on purpose'?
Sawsan won't take 'no' for an answer. She'll keep asking until she gets what she wants. She's _____.
In 'In general, I'm a very calm person', the phrase 'in general' is used to:
What was the role of Oliver's brother Monks in the story?
choices: Monks helped Oliver find Mr Brownlow
Monks ensured Oliver would become a criminal so he couldn't claim his inheritance
Monks was a police officer who tried to arrest Oliver
Monks was the manager of the workhouse

question: Rewrite using a participle clause: 'People who want to make an appointment should do so online.'
choices: People wanting to make an appointment should do so online.
People wants to make an appointment should do so online.
People to want to make an appointment should do so online.
People which wanting to make an appointment should do so online.

question: Read: 'Oliver decided to escape to London but had no food.' Why did Oliver decide to escape to London?
choices: because he wanted to become a thief
because he was unhappy with life in the workhouse and wanted to escape
because Fagin invited him to London
because Mr Brownlow was in London

question: Which phrase means 'a serious argument with a close friend that damages the relationship'?
choices: a newborn baby in the family
a row with your best friend

question: My sister _____ constantly be borrowing my things. She never asks!
Which phrase means 'to give someone too much praise and elevate them to an unrealistically high status'?
Read: 'I was an only child and I used to feel bored at home on my own.' Can we replace 'used to feel' with 'would feel' here?
choices: yes, because both describe past habits
no, because 'would' cannot describe a past state
yes, because 'would' can replace 'used to' in all cases
no, because 'used to' is only for actions, not states

question: Which type of relative clause uses commas and gives extra, non-essential information?
Which word means 'to fail an exam'?
She _____ always be the last to know about any news. (typical past annoying characteristic)
Which adjective describes someone who 'works very hard and puts great effort into their activities'?
London is a big city. It is the capital of Britain. (Combine using relative clause)
choices: London, who is the capital of Britain, is a big city.
London, where is the capital of Britain, is a big city.
London, which is the capital of Britain, is a big city.
London, whose is the capital of Britain, is a big city.

question: Which word means 'to have great respect and affection for someone based on their qualities'?
